def: s.f. A name of the Godavery river, or of the Tāpī.
adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

ety: S. तपस्; or S. ताप+ās = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Sunshine, the sunbeams; labour, toil.
more: s. f. 1. Sunshine, the sunbeams. 2. Toil, labour.

def: s.m. Heat; fire (esp. as an instrument of self-torture); pain, suffering, torture; religious austerity, penance mortification, the practice of mental or personal self-denial or the infliction of bodily torture; the meditation connected with such a practice; — moral virtue, merit; special observance or duty of any particular caste (e.g. the tapas of a Brāhman is sacred learning; of a Kshatriya, the protection of subjects; of a Vaiśya, giving alms to Brāhmans; of a Śūdra, service; and of a Rishi or saint, feeding upon herbs and roots); one of the seven worlds, inhabited by saints or devotees after death; — the hot season
more: n. f. 1. Claim; ground; footing; standing. 2. Combination; alliance; coalition; confederacy. ṭippas jamānā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To make or lay a foundation. 2. To make interest; secure one's good offices. 3. See panīrī jamānā .
s. (dakh.) Religious austerity, mortification of self, penance.
subs: s.m. The power acquired by religious austerities
adj. & s.m. Rich in religious penance; ascetic, pious; — one who treasures up the merit of mortifications and austerities, an ascetic, a devotee, one who performs religious penance
s.m. One of the seven worlds (see above)
s.m. 'Enclosure or district of religious penance,' a name applied to the holy land situated in central India
tapo-ban, s.m. 'Penance grove,' a sacred grove in which ascetics perform their religious austerities

def: s.f. corr. of tapasyā, q.v. s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
s. f. A female devotee.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
more: s.m. A devotee, a performer of austere devotion, an ascetick. 2. adj. Devout, practising austere devotion. تپسي مچهلي tapassī machhlī, s. f. The mango-fish. (Polynemus paradisea).

def: adj. & s.m. = tapasya, q.v.
subs: tapsī-maććhī, tapsī-maćh, s.f. The mango fish, Polynemus risua or Paradiseus.

def: = H تپشيا तपश्या tapaśyā, see tapassī, and tapasyā.
more: n. m. An ascetic; a devotee.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
ex: یہ تپسوی جنگل میں رہتے ہیں۔
ye tapasvī jaṅgal mẽ rahte ha͠i.
These ascetics live in the forest.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit तपस्वी (tapasvī).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
more: s. f. Devout austerity, religious penance, devotion.

def: adj. & s.m. Produced by heat; — the month Phāgun (Feb.-March); — religious penance, &c. ( = tapasyā).
= H تپشي तपश्शी tapaśśī, see tapassī, and tapasyā.
more: n. f. S. तप to heat. Religious penance. See H. تپ Bhāgīrath ne karī tapashshyā Har charnan se lagāyā man, Tab āīṅ brammāṅḍal se srī Gaṅgā jī tāran taran . Then Bhagīrat sacrificed, his mind on Harī's feet deep set, When from the mundane egg were Ganges' saving waters let.

def: adv. Without ceasing (the sound of falling drops, or the pattering of rain); one after another; successively. 1. Gup chup koṭhṛī meṅ baiṭhe haiṅ, āṅsū ṭap ṭap girte haiṅ . Alone her silent room she keeps, the tears keep coursing down her cheeks. 2. Ṭapā-ṭap lagīṅ golyāṅ bājne, Tillaṅgoṅ kī ṭopī lagī nāchne . Fast flew the bullets after one another, The soldiers' caps went dancing hither thither.

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.m. = H ٿپاٿوئي टपाटोई ṭapā-ṭoʼī, टप्पाटोईṭappā-ṭoʼī, s.f. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.f. = H ٿپاٿوا टपाटोआ ṭapā-ṭoʼā, टप्पाटोआṭappā-ṭoʼā, s.m. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).
s.m. Pouring out a libation; a libation.
v.t. = tapānā, q.v. v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).
adj. & s.m. Heated, glowing; oppressed by heat; suffering from disease (moral or physical); — anything heated or glowing.
more: s. f. Name of a river.

def: s.f. Name of the Surāt river (also called Tāptī); — a name of the Jamnā river.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
more: s. m. A devotee, a worshipper. See تپا tapā, and تپسي tapsī.

def: part. (of t̤apīdan), Agitated, distressed (esp. with heat), heated; palpitating, fluttering.
more: part. pass. (of طپيدن) Agitated, palpitating, flouncing, fluttering.

ety: rt. Zend tap+tan; S. तप्+तं; with conjunct. ī
def: adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
more: s. m. Palpitating, flouncing, agitation, tremour. تپيدن وار tapīdan-wār, adj. Agitated, troubled.

def: s.m. = tapīdan, q.v. adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
n. f. A hectic or chronic fever; phthisis; pulmonary consumption. tap kā mūt jānā , v. n. To break out (an eruption on the lips after a fever).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَپِ دِق (tap-i diq). By surface analysis, تپ + دق.

def: s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
more: (from تپيدن) s. f. 1. Affliction, agitation, palpitation. 2. Heat, warmth.

def: s.f. = tapish, q.v. s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
part. Heated, inflamed; distressed, pained.
s.m. Continuous dropping, dripping; a drop (of rain, &c., cf. ṭipkā); fruit falling when ripe (particularly mangoes), a windfall
more: s. m. 1. A drop of rain. 2. Fruit falling when ripe (particularly mangos), a windfall.
subs: s.f. Continuous dropping, dripping, trickle; a continuous dropping or falling off (as of fruit, or customers, or of men or animals under epidemic or murrain)
(-kā), To drip; to become leaky, to leak.

def: n. m. 1. Dropping; dripping; trickling; leak. 2. Falling of ripened fruit (particularly mangoes); a windfall. 3. Sprinkling any color with the fingers. ṭapkā-ṭapkī , n. f. 1. Dropping; trickling; drizzle. 2. Dropping (of fruit, customers, etc.) one after another. 3. Dying one after another. Ādmiyoṅ kī ṭapkā ṭapkī lag rahī hai . Men are dropping off one after the other. ṭapkā lagnā , v. n. To leak; become leaky. Āmoṅ kā ṭapkā lag rahā hai .
v. a. See تَپکنا
v.t. To cause to drop or drip, to distil.
more: (causal of ٿپکنا) v. a. To cause to drip, to distil.

ety: rt. of ṭapkānā+āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Dripping; distillation.
n. m. Dripping; distillation.
s.m. See s.v. tap. s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
more: s. m. Breaking out on the lips after a fever, the thrush.
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.

ety: ṭapak, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Friskiness, skittishness
more: s. f. diminutive of ٿپکا ṭapkā, q. v. ٿپکي پڙني عورت کي ṭapkī paṛnī aﻌurat kī, To be wanton (a woman).
subs: (-kī), To become frisky or skittish, to become wanton (a woman).

ety: tāp˚ = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S.अनीयं
def: v.t. To heat, warm, warm oneself (at or over a fire, e.g. āg tāpnā); — v.n. To bask (in the sun or before a fire).
more: v. n. To warm one's self at (or over) a fire or in the sun. 1. Jidhar jaltā dekheṅ, tidhar hī ko tāpeṅ . Prov. Where'er a fire she haps to see to warm herself she squats quite free. 2. Phūṅkne ke na phāṅkne ke, ṭāṅg uṭhā-ke tāpne ke . Prov. She 'll neither blow nor stir the fire, but she 'll up with her legs to warm o'er the fire. 3. Āg tāpeṅ kahāṅ talak insāṅ, Dhūp khāveṅ kahāṅ talak jāṅdār? Gālib. (The poet humorously expostulates with the king of Dehli in verse regarding his arrears of Pension). How long before the fire my naked body warm, Or wanting coals sit in the sun to keep just warm? 4. Kisī kā ghar jale, aur koī tāpe . Prov. One man's house burns, another warms himself by the fire.

v. n. 1. To paw (a horse). 1. Na khāve, na pīve, na sove kabhī, Na ṭāpe, na bīmār hove kabhī . The winged horse. Mīr Hasan. Nor eats, nor drinks, nor ever sleeps, Nor paws, nor ails, his course he keeps. 2. To stamp with impatience; to be imtient. 3. To cool one's heels; to be kept waiting. 4. To be kicking about; to saunter idly; to find oneself too late. Āj akele hī ṭāp rahe ho . 5. To stand gaping; to be petrified; to be lost in vain regrets. Yār chalā gayā, maiṅ ṭāptā rah gayā . 6. To spring; scale; leap over. Āj kal to divāleṅ ṭāpte ho! Now-a-days you are given to scaling walls. (He slips into womens' apartments). 7. To look about or search in vain. Woh naukrī ke vāste mahīnoṅ se ṭāp rahā hai .
v. a. To bask in the sun or before a fire, to warm oneʼs self at (or over) a fire, to warm, to heat.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀇 (tappaï), from Sanskrit तप्य॑ति (tápyati). Cognate with Punjabi ਤਪਣਾ (tapṇā) / تَپݨا (tapṇā), Sindhi تَپَڻُ (tapaṇu) / तपणु, Gujarati તાપવું (tāpvũ), Marathi तापणे (tāpṇe), Sinhalese තපිනවා (tapinawā).

ety: tap˚ = Prk. तप्प(इ) = S. तप(ति) or तप्य(ति), rt. तप्
def: v.n. To be heated; to glow; to burn with pain or grief; to frisk about; to shine, to be glorious.
more: v. n. 1. To be heated; to glow. Jitnā tapegā utnā barsegā . Prov. As the heat so will be the Rains. 2. To shine; to be glorious; to be held in awe. Rāj taptā hai . 3. To burn with pain or grief.
v. n. See الانگنا 1, 3. 1. To frisk; gambol. 2. To cover; superpose. 3. To top; mount; ride; cross; copulate.
v. a. 1. To jump across; clear. 2. To top; cover; pair.
v. n. 1. To be glorified. 2. To glow, to be heated, to frisk about.

def: v.n. To cover, to superpose; to put the hand (upon), pass the hand (over); to top, mount; to copulate, couple, pair (as birds, &c.); to jump (over or across), to vault; to jump about, be skittish, to frisk, gambol
more: v. a. To jump over. ٿپا ٿوا ṭapā ṭoʼā, part. Groping.
subs: (-ko), To spring or vault (over), to leap or jump (over or across); to jump down (-meṅ, upon, or in the midst of); (fig.) to thrust oneself (into the concerns of another), to intermeddle, interfere

def: v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse); to beat the feet on the ground, to stamp (in impatience, or pain, or despair, &c.), to be impatient, restless, or agitated; to tramp about; — v.t. To leap or spring over (a wall, &c.), to scale ( = ṭapnā).
more: v. n. 1. To paw with the fore feet (as, a horse that is impatient). 2. To whore. (Low phrase).

def: s.m. See s.v. tapas for compounds of which tapo is the first member. adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

s.m. Spring, leap, jump (in measure); bound, range (of a ball, bullet, &c.); distance, intervening space, interval (cf. pallā); a long stitch (in sewing); a relay, post (of ḍāk-bearers, post-runners, &c.); station for relays, post-station, stage, halting or resting-place; a post office; a raft, a catamaran (cf. ṭāpā); a mode (in music); a kind of hook; a small tract or division of country (smaller than a pargana, and consisting, generally, of a large town with villages and lands dependent; — ṭappā ḍālnā (-meṅ), To put in long stitches, to use long stitches, to baste
more: s. m. 1. The postoffice. 2. The name of a mode in musick. 3. The bound of a ball, &c. 4. A sort of hook. 5. A jump, a bound. ٿپا مارنا ṭappā-mārnā, 1. To sew or stitch with intervals. 2. To read in a desultory manner. ٿپا کهانا ṭappā khānā, To bound or ricochet (a shot).
subs: v.n. To bound or ricochet (a ball)
v.n. To bound; to make a long jump, take a long leap; to skip (in reading), to read in a desultory way; to baste ( = ṭappā ḍālnā).

def: s.m. = ٿپا ṭappā, q.v.
s.m. properly ṭappā, q.v.
more: n. m. 1. Bound; leap; spring (a ball, etc.). 2. Distance; reach; range; shot. Do golī ke ṭappe par jā paṛā . 3. A relay; a ḍāk . 4. A mode in music. 5. A raft; a catamaran. 6. A kind of hook. (?) 7. E. A small tract or division of country smaller than a Parganā , comprising one or more villages. ṭappā khānā , v. n. To bound or ricochet. ṭappā mārnā , v. n. 1. To baste (E. laṅgar denā ). 2. To read in a desultory way.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A post-station; post-office; post, mail; a relay (of runners, &c.)
more: s. (dakh.) A packet or mail (of letters, Harris ); the post.
subs: s.m. A runner, letter-carrier, postman ( = ṭappālī; ḍāk-wālā).

def: s.m. A letter-carrier, postman ( = ṭappāl-wālā).
s. m. (dakh.) Post-man.
n. f. Searching about; groping.
s.m. A thatched house, a hut, &c. (see ṭāpā).
n. m. A thatched house; a hut.

ety: tap˚ = thāp, q.v.+rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A mound; a small eminence or hillock, an acclivity; a limitary dike ( = meṅṛ).
more: s. f. A mound, a small height, an acclivity.

def: s.m. Plots of land (divided by means of taprīs), land cultivated in small patches among the uncultivated portions of an estate.
adj. = tapta and tattā, qq.v. part. adj. Heated, inflamed; hot, fervent, burning or burnt with heat, pain, or sorrow
subs: s.m. A kind of penance, drinking hot water, milk, or ghī for three days.

def: part. adj. Heated, inflamed; hot, fervent, burning or burnt with heat, pain, or sorrow
more: 1. adj. Hot, warm, fervent. 2. s. f. Heat.
subs: s.m. A kind of penance, drinking hot water, milk, or ghī for three days.

def: v.n. To drop continually, to drip.
s.f. Heat, &c. (see tapish).
s.m. An island; a shoal ( = ćar).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀝𑀸𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀼𑀓 (*ṭāppuka).

def: n. m. S. द्वीप An island (jazīrā); an isle.
s.m. A mineral substance, sulphuret of iron (said to be obtained from the river Tāpī).

ety: S. तन्त्र; and cf. S. तारः
def: s.m. Thread, string; the warp or threads extended lengthwise in a loom; wire, cord, string of a musical instrument; uninterrupted series, line, continuation, succession; a letter-file; the electric telegraph ( = ṭār-barqī); a telegraphic message, telegram; a hair of a curl or ringlet; the hair-worm or guinea worm; the snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the antennæ or feelers of prawns, flies, &c.; a bit, piece
more: n. m. S. तन to stretch. 1. A thread; string. 2. The warp. 3. A wire; a string of a musical instrument. 4. A letter file. 5. The electric telegraph ( tār-barqī ). 6. A telegram. Tār āyā hai . 7. The guinea worm ( nārū ). 8. The snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the horns, antennæ or feelers of prawns. 9. One hair of a curl or ringlet. 10. Continuation; succession; line; series. Kuchh bolī ṭholī piyār-bharī, kuchh gālī hai, kuchh mālī hai; In charchoṅ kā, in chohloṅ kā ye tār lagāyā Holī ne . Nazīr. Here honeyed words of amorous pairs, there wanton chaff and themes, Such sweet discourse, such prurient jokes doth Holi pour in streams. tār baṅdhnā , v. n. To be continued or repeated without interruption. Kyā hī chaman meṅ shab ko wāh barse thi nūr kī jhaṛī, Tār nashoṅ ke the baṅdhe, loṭe thī chāṅdnī paṛī . Nazīr. How silvery bright with moon beams showered the flower-deck…

n. m. S. ताल् A palm tree; the Palmyra or Borassus flabelliformis , and the Coryphatolliera . The name is most appropriate to the Palmyra, from the stem of which is extracted the juice which becomes tāṛī , or vulgarly, toddy. The corypha does not yield the same, but its leaves are used for writing upon. tāṛ-ban . A plantation of palm trees; a grove or 'tope' of palmyra trees. tāṛ-pattar . The palm-leaf prepared for writing, or a document so written.

s. m. f. 1. A thread. 2. A wire, the string of a musical instrument, a chord. 3. Darkness, obscurity. 4. The warp or threads placed lengthwise in weaving a piece of cloth. تارباندهنا tār bāndhnā, To continue or repeat an action without interruption. تارٿوٿنا tār ṭūṭnā, To be disjointed, to be separated. تارعنکبوت tār-i-aﻌnkabūt, Cobweb. تارکش tār-kash, s. m. A wiredrawer. تارکشي tār-kash-ī, s. f. 1. A kind of needle work. 2. Wiredrawing. تارکشي کرنا tār-kash-ī karnā, To draw wires, to wiredraw.

def: s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm, Borassus flabelliformis; the tree Corypha taliera (the leaves of which are used for writing upon)
more: s. m. The palm tree (Borassus flabelliformis).
(from تاڙنا) s.f. Understanding. تاڙباز tāṛbāz, adj. Intelligent, quick of apprehension. تاڙبازي tāṛ-bāz-ī, s. f. Quickness of apprehension.
s. m. The palmyra tree or fan palm.
subs: vulg. tāṛ-pattar, s.m. The palm-leaf prepared for writing on; a document written on palm-leaves
s.m. The gelatinous pulp of the fruit of the palmyra tree.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀸𑀟 (tāḍa), from Sanskrit ताड (tāḍa).

ety: Zend tauruna; S. taruṇ, rt. tr̤ī
def: adj. New, fresh; green; young, tender, soft; juicy, moist, damp, wet, wet through, saturated (with moisture, or grease, &c.); refreshed, revived, gladdened; — loose, large (as shoes, &c.)
more: adj. 1. Moist; wet; saturated. 2. Fresh; juicy; ripe. 3. Greasy; rich. Tarmāl jalebī! Street cry. 4. Refreshed; revived; gladdened. 5. Large; loose. Yeh jūtī zarā tar hai . 6. The sign of the Comparative degree. tar-ba-tar ; E. tartarātā , adj. 1. Completely wet; dripping; saturated. 2. Very greasy; very rich. tar zamīn , n. f. Moist land. tar k , v. a. more: s. m. 1. Safety. تران کرنا trāṅ karnā, To free, to protect. 2. A coat of mail.

def: s.m. Passing over, crossing; being saved, escape, deliverance; Svarga or paradise (the final landing-place); a raft; a boat; — one who is saved or delivered
subs: s.m. The saved and the saviour.

def: s. (dakh. for طرح t̤arḥ in the plur.) Modes, sorts.

adj. & s.m. Causing or enabling to cross; helping over a difficulty, &c.; liberating, saving; — who or what causes or enables to cross, one who delivers, deliverer, saviour; a raft, float; the bamboo framework of the roof of a house on which the thatch is laid; — crossing, passing over, reaching the opposite shore; salvation, deliverance
more: n. The bamboo frame of the roof of a house before the thatch is put on.
s. m. 1. The act of freeing, salvation, deliverance. 2. One that sets free or delivers.
subs: s.m. The saviour and the saved
adj. & s.m. Delivering, saving; — deliverer, saviour
v.t. To enable to cross; to save, &c. = tārnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Striking, beating, whipping, chastising; correction, chastisement, punishment; penalty; admonition, reproof, rebuke
more: (r. तड Beat) s. m. Punishment, admonition, reproof, penalty. تاڙناکرنا tāṛnā karnā, To reprove, punish, chastise.
subs: (or tāṛnā) karnā (-ko, or -kā), to reprove, rebuke, admonish; to correct, punish, &c.
v.t. To reprove, &c. ( = tāṛan karnā); to drive away, turn out.

def: s.m. Modulation, melody, harmony, symphony; voice, song, tune, air; trill, shake, quaver; a kind of song; an exercise in singing
more: s. m. 1. Modulation, harmony, voice, song, melody, symphony, trill, shake, quaver. 2. A kind of song. ترانه پرداز tarāna-pardāz, Composing songs.
subs: adj. & s.m. Composing melodies or songs; a composer of melodies, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَانَه (tarāna).

def: n. m. 1. Modulation; melody; harmony; symphony; a tune; an air. 2. An exercise in singing; a kind of song.
s.m. A raft or float (made of bamboos, &c. tied together, and sometimes floated upon jars or hollow gourds inverted); the float of a fishing line.
s.f. A raft, &c. ( = taranḍ); a boat, ship.
s.f. Wave, billow, ripple; emotion; rapture, transport, ecstasy; fancy, conceit, whim, caprice; becoming state and dignity.
more: n. f. 1. A wave. 2. See اُچنگ Kuchh āī jo us mah ke jī meṅ taraṅg, Kahā āj koṭhe pe bichchhe pilang! Mīr Hasan. 3. Transport; rapture; ecstasy. 4. Whim; fancy; conceit; caprice.
s. f. 1. Wave, emotion. 2. Whim, conceit, fancy, caprice. 3. Becoming state and dignity.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit तुरङ्ग (turaṅga).

def: s.m. An ornament for the ear ( = tāṭank, q.v.). s.m. An ornament for the ear, a large sort of ear-ring ( = tāṛaṅk).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀸𑀟 (tāḍa), from Sanskrit ताड (tāḍa).

def: adj. & s.m. Wavy, moving like a billow; moving restlessly to and fro, unsteady; fanciful, whimsical, capricious; fantastical; braggart; — an unsteady person; a whimsical person; a braggart.
s.f. A river.
part. adj. Wavy; overflowing (cf. tarangī).
s.m. = taranjubīn, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Passing through, pervading (space, &c.); — the sun; a ray of light.
more: s. f. A ship, a vessel, a boat.

def: s.f. A float, raft, boat; the plant Aloe perfoliata; the plant Hibiscus mutabilis.
s.f. A whip.
adj. Deserving of punishment, correction, admonition or reproof; punishable; reprovable.
s.m. An ornament for the ear ( = tāṭank, q.v.). s.m. An ornament for the ear, a large sort of ear-ring ( = tāṛaṅk).

ety: inf. n. v of رنم 'to trill or quaver'
def: s.m. Trilling, quavering, singing, modulating; song, modulation, rhythm.
more: (inf. v. of رنم) s. m. A song, modulation.

def: s.m. The ocean; a heavy shower, a torrent of rain; a fog.
s.f. A boat, vessel, ship ( = taraṇī).
adj. S. त्रिनवति Ninety-three.
s.f. = taṛap, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. Throb; flutter; palpitation. 2. A jump; leap; bound; spring. 3. A flash (of lightning).

ety: S. तट, or तड+स्फर; cf. the form taṛphaṛ-ānā
def: s.f. Tossing or rolling about restlessly or uneasily, feverish disquietude (of body or mind); agitation, flutter; throb, palpitation; anxious eagerness or desire; haste, hurry; leap, jump, bound, spring; glitter, flash (of lightning); crack, report, explosion; violence, fury, outrageousness.
more: (from تڙپنا) s. f. Haste, hurry, outrageousness, explosion, fury, leap, jump.

def: s.f. = taṛap, q.v.
(from تڙپهنا) s. f. Agitation, tossing about, palpitation.
s. f. See ٿڙپهـ taṛaph.

v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).

v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
v. n. See ٿڙپنا taṛapnā.

v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
more: taṛaphnā, v. n. 1. To flutter, palpitate, flounce, be agitated, writhe, jump, spring, bound, wriggle, toss. 2. To be very desirous about any thing.
taṛaphnā, v. n. To quiver, vibrate.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀟𑀧𑁆𑀨𑀟𑀇 (taḍapphaḍaï).

def: n. f. Hesitation; delay.
s.m. = taṛeṛā, q.v.
more: s. m. Throwing water. See تريڙا.

def: v. n. (dakh.) To rear (as, a horse).
s.m. (local), A hyena.

ety: Zend thwareś, fr. thwakhsh = S. त्वक्ष्
def: s.f. Cutting, hewing, paring, clipping; shaving; cut, shape, form, fashion (of clothes, &c.), make, build; — part. adj. & s.m. Cutting, chiselling, fashioning, &c.; — cutter, carver, fashioner, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. but-tarāsh, 'Image-carver')
more: n. f. تراشيدن to shave; pare. Cut; shape; form; fashion (of clothes); make; mould; build.
(from تراشيدن) 1. part. in compos. Cutting; cutter, carver. 2. s. f. Cutting, cut, paring, shape, form, fashion (of clothes, &c.). تراش ڐالنا tarāsh ḍālnā, To pare off or away.
subs: s.f. Shaping and fashioning; arranging (dress, &c.) neatly or elegantly; elegance of dress, &c.; fine or neat shape or form; — scratching out, erasure; — tarāsh-ḵẖarāsh-karnā, v.t. To make (oneself, apne taʼīṅ) spruce, to dress or attire oneself neatly or elegantly; — to scratch out, erase (writing, a word, &c.).

def: n. f. 1. Erasures; alterations; corrections ( chhīl chhāl ). 2. Fine shape or form; elegance in dress, coiffure, etc. tarāsh kharāsh karnā , v. a. 1. To scratch out; erase; alter; amend. 2. To get oneself up very smart.
more: s. f. Shape (particularly, good), neatness of form.

ety: Zend thwarsta; S. त्वष्ट; see tarāsh
def: s.m. lit. 'What is pared off,' &c.; paring, shaving, chip, splinter, clip, shred, scrap.
more: s. m. Shaving, paring, splinter, chip, shred, scrap.

def: v.t. To cut, hew, pare, clip, prune; to cut out, carve, shape, form, fashion; to shave (the beard, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. To cut; clip; slice; prune. 2. To cut out; carve; shape. 3. To coin or use (big words). Lugat tarāshnā. apne taīṅ tarāshnā , v. n. 1. To be a swell. 2. To value or esteem one's self above others.
(from تراش) v. a. 1. To cut, to clip, prune. 2. To shave. 3. To shape, to cut out. اپني تئين تراشنا apne taʼīṅ tarāshnā, To be vain, to value or esteem oneʼs self above others.
subs: To make oneself spruce, (vulg.) to come out a swell; to value or esteem oneself above others
v.t. (intens. of tarāshnā), To cut up, to pare off, pare away, &c. ( = ćhīl-ḍālnā).

def: v.n. To be pared, &c. (see tarāshnā).
more: v. n. To be pared. See تراشنا v. a.
v. n. To be cut or pared.

ety: inf. n. v of رشح 'to exude sweat,' &c.
def: s.m. Sweating, exuding, exudation; dropping, dripping, distilling; sprinkling, small rain, drizzle; — adj. Manifest, apparent
more: n. m. رشح exuded. 1. What is exuded, dropping (as sweat, etc.) 2. Small rain; drizzling. tarashshoh , adj. Manifest; evident. tarashshoh honā , Ped. for H. phuiyāṅ paṛnā , v. n. 1. To drizzle. 2. To appear; seem.
(inf. v. of رشح) s. m. Exuding, dropping (as, sweat, &c.). 2. Dripping, distilling. 3. A small rain.
subs: v.n. To drizzle ( = phuʼiyāṅ paṛnā); — to be apparent, to appear, seem.

def: v.t. To cause to be cut or pared, to have (a thing) cut, &c.
more: v. a. Caus. of tarāshnā q. v.
v. a. To cause to cut or pare.

def: v.n. To long, be eagerly or anxiously desirous (of), to crave or desire earnestly and repeatedly, to beg (for), entreat, supplicate; to be tantalized.
more: v. n. 1. To long; desire anxiously. Darshan bin aṅkhiyāṅ taras rahīṅ . Song. 2. To stand in need or want of; to lack.
v. n. 1. To long, to desire anxiously, be agog. 2. To pity.
v. n. (dakh.) To beseech, beg, entreat.

ety: inf. n. v of رصد 'to watch or wai' for
def: s.m. Watching or waiting (for), expectation hope.
more: (inf. v. of رصد) s. m. Expectation, hope.

ety: inf. n. v of رسل 'to become easy in pace'
def: s.m. Acting gently, deliberately, or leisurely; being distinct or proceeding slowly (in reading).
more: (inf. v. of رسل) s. Acting gently, being distinct or proceeding slowly (in reading).

def: s.m. Gradation, proportion, the state or condition of more or less; distinction, discrimination.
s. m. Garden-cresses. (Lepidium sativum).
n. m. Garden-cresses; Lepidium sativum . Wat.
s.m. = taṛāyā, q.v. s.m. Gaudiness (in dress), wearing smart clothes, foppishness; vanity, show, ostentation
subs: adj. & s.m. Showy, ostentatious, vain, foppish; — one who wears fine clothes, or is particular as to dress, &c., a fop.

ety: tar, q.v.+āwat = Prk. वत्ता = S. वार्त्ता
def: s.f. Freshness; greenness, verdure; juiciness; moisture, dampness, humidity ( = tarī).
s.f. = tarāwat (the correct form).

ety: for A. طراوة, inf. n. of طرو 'to be fresh, or juicy,' &c.
def: s.f. Freshness, juiciness, succulence; greenness, verdure; moisture, humidity, dampness.
more: n. f. 1. Moisture; humidity. 2. Freshness; verdure. Bāg ko dekh-kar āṅkhoṅ meṅ tarāvaṭ ā gaī .
s. f. Freshness, verdure; moisture, humidity, dampness.

def: s.f. vulg. corr. of the next, q.v.

ety: pl. of tarwīḥat, 'a single rest,' rt. روح
def: s.f. A form of prayer consisting of twenty or more genuflexions (according to different persuasions), performed at some period of the night in the month of Ramaẓān, after the ordinary prayers of nightfall (but, by pious persons, often repeated every morning: they are so called because the performer rests after every four genuflexions).
more: Mah. n. f. Certain extra prayers offered up by the more devout among the Moslems.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَاوِیح (tarāwīh), from Arabic تَرَاوِيحْ (tarāwīḥ).

more: s. f. Distillation, dripping, trickling, exudation.

def: s.m. Gaudiness (in dress), wearing smart clothes, foppishness; vanity, show, ostentation
more: s. m. Gaudiness in dress, wearing smart clothes. تڙايا دار taṛāyā-dār, s. m. One who dresses well and is particular in having fine and well made clothes.
subs: adj. & s.m. Showy, ostentatious, vain, foppish; — one who wears fine clothes, or is particular as to dress, &c., a fop.

more: s. f. in compos. Adorning, ornamenting.

more: n. f. Z. tarāzūk ; A. mīzān , S. तुला. 1. A pair of scales; a balance. tarāzū honā , v. n. lit . to be put in the scales. To be finely balanced; to hit the mark. Tir tarāzū ho gayā .
s. f. A scale, a balance.
subs: (-meṅ), To be evenly balanced; to go quite through, or to pierce (an object), and be out equally on both sides (an arrow, &c.); to hit the mark.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترازو (tarāzū).

s.m. = tarwar, q.v. s.f. A sword ( = tarwār)

v.t. To strike or cut with a sword, to use a sword
adv. 'Under the shadow of swords'; well-guarded.

more: (inf. ii. of ربع) s. f. 1. Dividing into four, making a quadrangular or square figure. 2. (in astronomy) A quadrangular aspect of the stars, a quartile, quadrature.

s. (dual of طرف) Both sides, both parties.
subs: The two ends of a chain; the extreme terms of a series.

def: The twinkling of an eye.
more: (prop. t̤arfatu-l-aﻌin ) The twinkling of an eye.

def: telegraph office
wety: From تار (tār, “wire, telegraph”) + گھر (“place”).

ety: inf. n. ii of رغب 'to desire'
def: s.f. Exciting desire, incitement, instigation, stimulation, incentive; inducement; temptation, allurement, lure, bait
more: n. f. رغبت desiring greatly. 1. Exciting desire; inducement; encouragement; incitement; instigation; stimulation. 2. Allurement; lure; sop; bait; decoy. targīb denā , v. a. 1. To persuade; induce; encourage; incite; entice; excite. 2. To allure; lure; tempt; cajole; decoy. 3. To put up to; influence; set on; instigate; abet.
(inf. ii. of رغبت) s. f. Exciting desire, incitement, instigation, temptation, stimulation. ترغيب دينا targ̠ẖīb denā, To set agog, excite, incite, to tempt, entice, induce.
subs: (-kī), To stimulate, incite, instigate, excite, induce; to entice, tempt, allure.

ety: inf. n. of طرح 'to throw, cast,' &c.
def: s.f. Throwing; ejection; laying (a foundation); fixing (a position, &c.); foundation (of a building, &c.); position, establishment, location; plan, design; form, description, sort, kind; manner, mode; air (syn. andāz); state, condition (syn. ḥāl); a verse set, showing the particular metre in which a poem is to be composed
more: n. f. See chhab . 1. From; description ( bhāṅt ). 2. A plan; design. 3. Air; manner ( andāz , 3). 4. State ( hāl , 1). 5. Foundation; base. 6. A line or verse set, shewing the metre in which the poem is to be written. Kaho ab ke kyā tarah huī hai? tarah ba-tarah, tarah tarah kā , adj. Of various sorts; diversified ( bhāṅt bhāṅt kā ). Tarah ba-tarah ke phūl khil rahe the. tarah-dār , adj. Stylish; graceful; handsome. tarah-dārī , n. f. Style; grace. tarah denā , v. a. 1. To disregard; overlook; pass over. 2. To turn a deaf ear to; give the go-bye. tarah, yā andāz dikhānā , v. a. To show off one's airs and graces; to affect. is tarah se, is tarah . Accordingly. is tarah jab tak ki ye hāl rahe. Toties quoties. burī tarah, be-tarah , adv. Badly; severely; shamefully; disgracefully. kis tarah , adv. In what way; how. kisī tarah , adv. Anyhow; somehow or other. kaī tarah se . In many respects …

s. f. 1. Manner, mode. 2. Position, establishment. 3. Ejection, throwing. طرح بطرح t̤arḥ ba-t̤arḥ, Various sorts. طرحدار t̤araḥ-dār, adj. Beautiful, graceful, well-shaped. طرحداري t̤araḥ-dār-ī, s. f. Beauty, elegance. طرح کش t̤arh-kash, Sketching, imitating.
subs: adj. Well-shaped, beautiful, graceful:-t̤araḥ-dārī, s.f. Beauty, elegance, grace, style
To exhibit (one's) airs and graces; to affect
(or t̤araḥ) denā (-ko), To throw away, cast aside; to disregard, overlook, pass over, or by, unnoticed; to turn a deaf ear (to)
adj. Drawing a plan (of), designing; sketching; imitating
adv. In this way, thus, accordingly
adv. In a bad way, badly, severely, shamefully, disgracefully
In what manner? how? — kisī t̤araḥ, adv. Anyhow; somehow or other
adv. In many ways; in many respects.
ex: یہ کس طرح سے بنا ہے؟
ye kis tarah se banā hai?
By what method is this made?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طرح (tarh, “plan, design”), from Arabic طَرْح (ṭarḥ, “teaching, deduction”).

def: v. n. (dakh.) See تڙپهنا taṛaphnā.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Enabling or causing to get over or across; saving; — saviour, deliverer ( = tāraṇ).
more: n. f. Palm juice or toddy.
s. f. 1. The clapping of hands. 2. A key. 3. Abstraction, absence of mind, absorption in thought or devotion.

def: s.f. The juice of the palmyra-tree, palm-wine, toddy; — the hilt of a dagger; — slapping the arms in defiance (in wrestling)
more: s. f. The juice of the palm tree. (Corrupt. Toddy).
s. f. Hilt of a dagger.
subs: v.n. To slap the arms in defiance
s.m. The man who climbs palmyra trees and extracts the juice or toddy from them.
wety: From تاڑ (tāṛ, “palmyra, toddy palm”) + ـی (-ī), ultimately from Dravidian.

def: s.f. Beating, punishment; loss; cheating, fraud, deceit.
s.f. A small axe, a hatchet.

ety: act. part. of طرأ or طرو 'to come unexpectedly' (upon
def: part. adj. Coming unexpectedly, intervening, happening
more: part. act. (of طري) Happening, occurring, intervening.
subs: (-par), To come unexpectedly (on or over), to overcome, overshadow.

def: v. n. To prevail over; overcome; overshadow. Aisī dahshat tārī huī ki bol na sakā .
s.f. Darkness, &c. ( = tārīkī, q.v.). s.f. Darkness, obscurity.

s.f. Freshness, juiciness, ripeness; moisture, dampness, humidity, wetness; water (in opp. to ḵẖushkī, 'dry land'); low lands on the bank of a river, &c., land covered with water, moist soil, marsh, swamp; — sugar; — tari-se, adv. By water
subs: s.f. & adv. Water-route, journey by water; — by water.
wety: From Sanskrit त्रि (tri, “three”).

def: s.f. A key, &c. ( = tālī, q.v.); — absence of mind, abstraction, absorption in thought or devotion.
s.f. A boat.
more: n. f. 1. Moisture; dampness; wet. 2. Water (opposite of barrī land). 3. Freshness; juiciness; ripeness. 4. Low lands on the banks of rivers. 5. Moist land; swamp; marsh. tarī kī rāh, tarī kā safar , n. m. Passage by sea; a voyage.
n. f. 1. Beating; punishment. 2. Cheat; deceit; gammon. 3. Loss.
adj. Three. 2. tare, adv. Under: see تلي tale.
s. f. 1. Moisture, freshness, water (in opposition to dry land). 2. Sugar. تري سي tarī se, adv. By water.
wety: From Sanskrit त्रि (tri, “three”).

def: s.f. Distance, remoteness, interval (syn. antar; tafāwut).

ety: inf. n. ii of عرف 'to know'
def: s.f., lit. 'Making known'; explaining (a term, &c.), explanation; description; making determinate, defining, a definition; introduction (to a person); praising, praise, commendation; — a table of rates of export and import duties (hence the European 'tariff')
more: n. f. عرف knew. 1. A definition (of a term); a description. 2. Praise; commendation; laudation. 3. An introduction to a person. See اوصاف 4. (Eng. tariff) A table of rates of export and import duties, in which sense the word has been adopted in English and other European languages (probably through the Spanish from the Arabic). tārīf-ul-majhūl bil-majhūl . Explaining in terms as little understood as the thing intended to be explained.

(inf. ii. of عرف) s. f. 1. Explanation, description. 2. Praise, praising. 3. Assertion. تعريف المحجـﮩول بالمحجـﮩول taﻌrīfu-l-majʼhūl bi-l-majʼhūl, Explaining in terms as little understood as the thing intended to be explained.
subs: 'Explaining the unknown by the unknown,' explaining in terms as little understood as the thing intended to be explained.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْرِیف (ta'rīf), borrowed from Arabic تَعْرِيف (taʕrīf), from عَرَّفَ (ʕarrafa).

def: s.f. pl. (of taʻrīf), Definitions, &c.
adj. Commendable; notable.
part. adj. & s.m. Leaving, forsaking, abandoning, relinquishing; — a deserter
more: (from ترک) part. act. Leaving; a deserter. تارک الدنيا tāriku-d-dunyā, 1. adj. Abandoning the world, abstinent, sober, chaste. 2. s. m. An anchoret, a hermit.
subs: adj. & s.m. Abandoning the world; abstinent, sober, chaste; — an anchorite, a recluse, hermit.

ety: Pehl. tārīk; fr. Z. tãthra, rt. tam = S. tam
def: adj. Dark, obscure
more: adj. Dark. تاريک چشم tārīk chashm, adj. Blind, dim-sighted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تاریک (tārīk).

def: s.m. A ferry-man; a raft, float.
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: (v.n. fr. طرق 'to beat,' &c.), s.m. & f. 'A beaten track,' a road, way, path, course; mode, manner, fashion, &c. ( = t̤arīqa, q.v.).
more: n. f. طرق made away. 1. Way; road. 2. Manner ( ḍhaṅg , 1). 3. Custom; fashion ( chāl , 4.). 4. Rites; observances.
(from طرق) s. f. 1. Way, road. 2. Manner, custom, fashion. 3. Rite, religion. (Plur. طرق t̤uruḳ ).

def: s.m. Fare, freight, toll for passage.

r: t̤arīqa, t̤arīqat, t̤arīq, ahl-ě-tarīqat, ahl-ě-t̤arīqa, Sūfīs
def: s.m. = P طريقت t̤arīqat, s.f. (for A. طريقة, fr. t̤arīq, q.v.), A road, path, way, course, rule, mode of acting, &c., manner of conduct, &c., custom, fashion, established rule or usage; system; — religious ordinance, religion; sect; rite, observance; — the better sort (of people), the most excellent or the best (of a people, &c.)
more: s. m. 1. Way, path. 2. Manner, mode. 3. Religion, sect. 4. The better sort of people.
subs: s.m. pl. Devout or religious persons; devotees; the sect of Sūfīs.
ex: نیا کام کرنے کا طریقہ اپنانا چاہیے۔
nayā kām karne kā tarīqā apnānā cāhiye
You should adopt a new method of working.
اِس بات کرنے کا طریقہ صحیح نہیں ہے۔
is bāt karne kā tarīqā sahīh nahī̃ hai.
This manner of talking is not correct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَرِیقَه (tarīqa), from Arabic طَرِيقَة (ṭarīqa).

def: s.m. A legacy, bequest; inheritance; inheritance by succession or bequest; effects or estate of a deceased person
more: n. m. ترکه left. 1. A bequest; a legacy. 2. Inheritance; effects of the deceased; inheritance by succession or bequest; the estate or property of a deceased person. tarkah bilā vasīat-nāma . Intestate property. tarkah pāne-wālā , n. A legatee. tarkah-i-pidrī ; H. bapautī . Paternal estate. tarke meṅ ānā . To inherit; succeed to; descend; come into possession. tarke meṅ chhoṛnā . To leave; bequeath.
s. m. The estate of a deceased person.
subs: v.n. To come as a bequest, be obtained as an inheritance; — to descend (to); to succeed (to); to come into possession
v.t. To leave as a legacy, to bequeath.

def: s.f. = tarika, q.v. s.m. Fare, freight, toll for passage.

r: t̤arīqat, t̤arīqa, t̤arīq, ahl-ě-tarīqat, ahl-ě-t̤arīqa, Sūfīs
def: s.f. = P طريقه t̤arīqa, s.m. (for A. طريقة, fr. t̤arīq, q.v.), A road, path, way, course, rule, mode of acting, &c., manner of conduct, &c., custom, fashion, established rule or usage; system; — religious ordinance, religion; sect; rite, observance; — the better sort (of people), the most excellent or the best (of a people, &c.)
more: s. f. 1. Way, path. 2. Manner, mode. 3. Religion, sect. 4. The better sort of people.
subs: s.m. pl. Devout or religious persons; devotees; the sect of Sūfīs.

ety: inf. n. ii of ارخ 'to date'; or, according to some, the Ar. form of Heb. יֶרַח or Ch. יְרַח 'a month'
def: s.f. Date, era, epoch; day (of a month); chronogram; chronicle, book of annals, history
more: n. f. ارخ dating a letter. 1. Date; day of the month. In recording dates of historical or memorable events the 28 letters of the Arabic language are generally used. Each of these has a certain numerical value attached, ranging from one to one thousand. 2. Epoch; era; chronology; annals; history; chronogram. tārīkh chaṛhānā , v. a. To enter the date; to date. tārīkh-wār , adv. According to date; at stated periods; in chronological order; periodically. falānī tārīkh se chaṛhtī . Calculated from a certain date.

(inf. ii. of obsol. ارخ He loosened, travelled: or, rather, from Heb. and Ch. A month) s. f. 1. Date, era, epoch, day (of a month); chronogram. 2. Annals, history. تاريخ الـﮩجره tārīḵẖu-l-hijra, The era of the Hijra. تاريخ وار tārīḵẖ-wār, adv. Daily, at stated periods.
subs: (-par), To put the date (on), to date
s.f. The era of the Hijra
adv. According to date, in chronological order; at stated periods, periodically; daily.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تاریخ (tārix), from Arabic تَارِيخ (tārīḵ).

def: history
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَارِیخْچَه (tārīxča), diminutive of تَارِیخ (tārīx).

def: historical
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَارِيخِيّ (tārīḵiyy) (likely through Classical Persian تاریخی (tārīxī)). By surface analysis, تاریخ (tārīx, “history”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. Darkness, obscurity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تاریکی (tārīkī).

def: immigrants; sacrificers
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تارکین (tārikīn), from the Arabic plural of تَارِك (tārik), active participle of تَرَكَ (taraka, “to leave”).

def: Ped. The sun's course. tarīqa batānā , v. a. 1. To shew the way; to direct. 2. To point out the proper course. tarīqa-i-baṭvārā . Mode of partition. tarīqa baratnā , G. G. To work a system. tarīqa-i-hisāb . System of account. tarīqa-i-darbār . Forms and ceremonies proper to a darbār or levee; forms of state. tarīqa-i-amal-dar-āmad . Procedure. tarīqa-i-muqarrara-i-qānūn . The mode prescribed by law. tarīqa-i-munāsib . The proper mode. ahl-i-tarīqat . Devout or religious people, esp. Sūfīs. ba-tarīq, ba-taur , adv. In the way of; by way of; as though. ba-tarīq-i-amānat . By way of deposit. ba-tarīq-i-muflisī. In forma pauperis ; as a pauper. adālat meṅ talab karne kā tarīq . How brought before a court. muqaddama pesh karne kā tarīq . How a suit is to be brought.

ety: tar, q.v.; īn = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Moist, fresh, &c. ( = tar, q.v.). s.m. Thread, string; the warp or threads extended lengthwise in a loom; wire, cord, string of a musical instrument; uninterrupted series, line, continuation, succession; a letter-file; the electric telegraph ( = ṭār-barqī); a telegraphic message, telegram; a hair of a curl or ringlet; the hair-worm or guinea worm; the snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the antennæ or feelers of prawns, flies, &c.; a bit, piece
subs: (-ke), To continue or repeat (an act) without interruption
s.f. The electric wire, electric telegraph; a telegram
(-ke), To take place uninterruptedly or continuously (an act), to be continued or repeated without interruption
adv. Piece by piece, piecemeal; distinctly; in pieces or shreds, tattered and torn
v.n. To speak in detail or distinctly
(-ke), To tear to pieces or into shreds
(-ke), To be broken in continuity; to be interrupted; to be disjointed; to be separated
s.m. A kind of needlework, open work; pulling out some threads, &c.
s.f. A kind of needlework, any fine cloth run with gold or silver thread
s.m. A drawer of gold or silver wire
tār-kashī. s.f. Wire-drawing; a kind of needlework
(-kā), To find or obtain a clue (to)

def: (see tar); an affix, the termination of the superlative degree in Persian (corresponding to the English termination -est, or the adverb 'most,' e.g. ḵẖūb-tarīn, 'fairest, most beautiful').
more: 1. A particle added to adjectives, to form the superlative degree; most, very. 2. adj. Moist, fresh, good.

def: s.f. Deliverer, saviour; a form of the goddess Durgā; name of a kind of goddess with Buddhists and Jains.
s.f. Lightning.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit तडित् (taḍít).

def: s.m. A boat.
s.m. A boat.

ety: inf. n. ii of عرض 'to be broad,' &c.
def: s.f. Making broad or wide, enlarging; making conspicuous; rendering obnoxious; opposing, objecting; ambiguity or equivocation in speech; hinting at a subject obscurely or indirectly, insinuation (of wrong, or evil, &c.), making an allusion (to); — rendering intricate; writing indistinctly or illegibly; not dressing meat sufficiently; bartering, exchanging, barter, &c.; — a statement of particulars of landed property (deposited in a collector's office).
more: (inf. ii. of عرض) s. f. 1. Enlarging. 2. Making conspicuous. 3. Rendering obnoxious. 4. Ambiguity in speech or hinting at a subject obscurely, insinuation of evil. 5. Rendering intricate. 6. Making writing illegible. 7. Not dressing meat sufficiently. 8. Opposing, objecting. 9. Barter, exchange of one commodity for another.

def: s.m. Interpretation; translation
more: n. m. ترجمه he interpreted in another language. Translation; version. tarjama k. , v. a. To translate; make a translation. lafzī tarjama . A literal translation.
s. m. Translation, interpretation. ترجمه کرنا tarjama karnā, To translate, to interpret. ترجمه نويس tarjuma-navīs, s. m. A translator. ترجمه هونا tarjama honā, To be interpreted or translated.
subs: s.m. A translator ( = mutarjim); — tarjama honā, v.n. To be interpreted or translated.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترجمه (tarjama), from Arabic تَرْجَمَة (tarjama).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
oblique plural of تَرْجَمَہ (tarjama, “translation”)
s.m. Threatening; blaming, censuring; pointing at in ridicule or contempt; wrath, anger.
s.m. = tarjan, q.v. s.m. Threatening; blaming, censuring; pointing at in ridicule or contempt; wrath, anger.
s.f. The fore-finger (as used for threatening).

ety: inf. n. ii of رجع 'to return,' &c.
def: s.f. Causing to return or recur, &c.
subs: s.m. A kind of stanza in which one line occurs at stated intervals. (It is, perhaps, only in this compound that the word tarjīʻ occurs in Urdū).

def: n. m. A. رجوع return, repetition. A stanza, in which one line recurs at stated intervals.
more: A kind of stanza, in which one line recurs at stated intervals. (From رجوع Return, repetition).

ety: inf. n. ii of رجح 'to excel; to outweigh,' &c.
def: s.f. Gaining a superiority; superiority, preference, excellence, pre-eminence, precedence
more: n. f. رجح the balance inclined, he excelled. Preference; superiority; pre-eminence; precedence. tarjīh denā , v. a. To prefer; give the preference to.

(inf. ii. of رجوح) s. f. Gaining a superiority, preference, excellence, preeminence. ترجيح بلامرجح tarjīḥ-i-bilā murajjaḥ, Unrea sonable preference. ترجيح دينا tarjīḥ denā, To prefer. ترجيح رکهنا tarjīḥ rakhnā, To excel, to surpass.
subs: v.t. To give (one thing, &c.) the preference (to, -par, another), to prefer (to)
(-par), To have precedence (over), to be superior (to), to excel, surpass.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرْجِیح (tarjīh), from Arabic تَرْجِيح (tarjīḥ).

def: v.t. To threaten, to terrify; to censure.
s.m. An interpreter.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترجمان (tarjumān), from Arabic تَرْجُمَان (tarjumān).

def: s.f. Interpreting; office of interpreter.

s.m. Supposition, conjecture; reasoning, speculation, inquiry, consideration, meditation, discussion, disputation; argument, objection, plea; doubt; the science of reasoning, logic; (in logic) a proposition
more: n. m. ترک he left it. 1. Relinquishment; abandonment; abdication; renunciation. 2. Omission. 3. A catchword. tark-i-adab , adj. 1. Disrespect; rudeness. 2. Boldness; presumption; presumptuousness. tark-i-dunyā . Renunciation of the world and its vanities or pleasures. tark karnā , v. a. To abandon. See تياگنا tark-i-vatan ; Ped. for H. des chhoṛan . Leaving one's native country; emigration. tark honā , v. n. To be in disuse, given up or abandoned.
n. m. 1. Objection; plea; argument. 2. Reasoning; logic; disputation; discussion. 3. (In logic.) A proposition. tark uṭhānā , v. a. To raise a plea or objection. tark shāstar, tark vidyā . Logic.

s. m. Abandoning, abandonment, omission, leaving, deserting, abdication. ترک ادب tark-i-adab, Disrespect. ترک کرنا tark karnā, To abandon, leave, forsake, leave off, quit, omit, cease, desert, resign.
s. m. 1. Logick. 2. (in logick) A proposition.
s. An iron helmet or cap worn in battle.
subs: v.n. To raise an objection or plea
s.m. = tark-vidyā, s.f. The science of reasoning or disputation, logic
v.t. To consider, investigate, reason, &c. ( = tarknā).
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: s.m. Abandoning, forsaking, leaving; setting aside; abandonment, desertion; relinquishment renunciation; abdication; omission, neglect; a catch-word
subs: s.m. Rudeness, incivility, disrespect; boldness, presumptuousness
v.t. To abandon, forsake, desert, leave, quit; to set aside; to give up, relinquish, renounce, resign; to leave off, desist from, cease (syn. tyāgnā)
s.m. Leaving one's native land, emigration
v.n. To be abandoned; to be given up; to be in disuse.
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: s.m. An iron helmet or cap (worn in battle).
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: v.n. To be day-break, to dawn; — to be broken, become bankrupt, to be reduced to poverty; to be roused, have the eyes opened, be brought to one's senses; to be astonished or astounded
more: s. m. Dawn of day. تڙکي taṛke, adv. In the morning early, at dawn of day.
subs: adv. At dawn or day-break, in the early morning, early.
wety: From تڑکنا (taṛaknā, “to crack, to break”).

def: s.f. Logical reasoning.
more: n. m. Dawn; day break. taṛke , adv. At dawn; in the early morning. taṛkā honā , v. n. 1. To be dawn. 2. To be bankrupt, reduced to proverty. Sau rupaë nikal jāne se taṛkā ho gayā . 3. To be brought to one's senses, roused. Aise piṭe ki taṛkā ho gayā . 4. To be stunned; astonished; at one's wit's end. nūr kā taṛkā , Early in the morning; dawn.

def: v.t. To put off, &c. = ṭālnā, q.v. v.t. To fry; — v.n. To be fried (in butter or oil).
v. n. See. ṭāl'nā , 1, 2, 3. Āj to be-vazū hī tarkāo . Slang. To put off; evade; shuffle; give the go-by. Sūkhā ṭarkāyā . Slang.
v.t. To cause to crack, &c.; to crack, split, burst; to snap, break.
more: v. a. To crack, split, burst, &c. ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Esculent vegetables; meat (in this sense used by Hindūs only).
more: n. f. Pahlvī. tarah ; Z. tarnekā . 1. Esculent vegetables ( tar-tarkārī ). 2. ( Hīn .) Meat. Tel takārī lā dū go ḍhebuā ḍhaile jā! Bhoj. Leave two pice for oil and vegetables. 2. A tax imposed on gardens or ground on which vegetables are grown, especially ginger, turmeric, tobacco, etc., on which a higher rate of assessment is usually levied.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تره (tara, “vegetable”) + Tamil கறி (kaṟi). Compare Assamese তৰকাৰী (torkari), Bengali তরকারী (torkari).

def: s.m. A quiver
more: n. m. A quiver. 1. Laṛeṅ na bhiṛeṅ, tarkash bāṅdhe phireṅ . Prov. With bow and arrow girt; but not for fight. 2. Tarkash meṅ do tīr nahīṅ, par sharmā sharmī laṛte haiṅ . Prov. Not an arrow left in his quiver, he fights on for very shame.
s. m. A quiver. ترکش بند tarkash-band, Wearing a quiver.

def: n. f. Wire drawing. tār-ghar , n. m. Telegraph office. tār-nikālnā , v. a. Wom. To find a clue.
adj. Swift, rapid (as a stream).
s.f. A contemptuous epithet for a woman; an old jade; a low-priced trull, a drab.
more: s. m. A contemptuous epithet for a woman, a low priced trull, a drab.

ety: inf. n. ii of رخم 'to become soft, or easy'
def: s.f. Curtailing, abbreviating; abbreviation, contraction, apocope.
more: n. f. رخم Cutting off at the end. ( Gram .) Cutting off the final letter or syllable; apocope.
(inf. ii. of رخم) s. f. Abbreviation, contraction, apocope (in grammar).

def: s.m. = tarkin, q.v. s.m. A follower of the Tark-śāstra; a logician, disputant, reasoner.
more: n. f. H. tāṛ palm tree. An ear-ring originally made of tāṛ leaves, worn by women.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترکی (turkī). By surface analysis, ترک (turk) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. A kind of ear-ring worn by women (made originally of the tāṛ leaf).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترکی (turkī). By surface analysis, ترک (turk) + ـی (-ī).

ety: inf. n. ii of رکب 'to overlie,' &c.
def: s.f. Putting together, combining, mixing; setting (a stone); composition; compound; mixture; construction, structure, make, mechanism; form, fashion, mode, method, arrangement; means, plan, contrivance
more: n. f. A. رکب overlaid. 1. Formation; construction; composition. 2. Form; fashion; mode. 3. ( Gram .) Analysis; parsing ( anve ). 4. Plan; scheme; arrangement. (Erroneously for tadbīr , q. v.) tarkīb-band , n. m. A stanza. tarkīb denā , v. a. 1. To make; construct; organize. 2. To form; shape; fashion; mould. tarkīb se , adv. 1. Economically; carefully. 2. Somehow or other; by hook or by crook; as best one can. tarkīb k .; H. anve k . v. n. To parse; analyse; construe. be-tarkīb , adv. Excessively; without measure. Be-tarkīb khāyā aur okā .

(inf. ii. of رکب) s. f. Composition, mixture, cement, make, form, fashion, mechanism. ترکيب بند tarkīb-band, A kind of poem. ترکيب دار tarkīb-dār, Well-formed. ترکيب کرنا tarkīb karnā, To organize. (Plur. ترکيبات tarkībāt, Mixtures, compositions).
subs: (kī), To exhibit the structure (of a clause or sentence), to coustrue, to analyse
s.m. A particular kind of metrical composition; a stanza
(-ko), To give form or shape (to), to form, fashion; to make, construct, organize
adv. In due proportion; in proper form, or measure, &c.; economically; carefully
(-kī), To construct, frame, organize; to construe, &c. ( = tarkīb batānā).

def: s.f. pl. (of tarkīb), Compositions; compounds; mixtures, &c.
adj. Composed; compounded, mixed; artful, artificial.
adj. & s.m. Related or belonging to the science of reasoning or logic; — a dialectician, logician, philosopher, sophist; a follower of one of the six schools of Hindū philosophy.

ety: inf. n. ii of رقّ 'to be thin,' &c.
def: s.f. Thinning; dilution; rarefaction; softening.
more: (inf. ii. of رقّت) s. f. Softening, thinning, dilution, rarefaction.

def: s.m. The tree Cassia tora.

ety: inf. n. ii of رقم 'to mark, stamp,' &c.
def: s.f. Marking, figuring (cloth, &c.); writing, noting.
more: (inf. ii. of رقم) s. f. Writing, noting.

def: s.m. A follower of the Tark-śāstra; a logician, disputant, reasoner.
part. adj. Investigated, examined, discussed; disputed, doubted; doubtful; disputatious; — s.m. A disputatious person.
v.t. To consider, investigate, reason or speculate about; to discuss, dispute, doubt, &c. (see tark).
more: v. a. To remonstrate; object; argue; dispute; discuss.
v. n. P. tarqīdan to split. 1. To be cracked, split. 2. To be angry, in a rage. Miyāṅ taṛakhte kyoṅ ho?

def: s.m. A kind of bird; a kind of plant; also = next, q.v.
more: n. m. A drawer of gold or silver wire.

def: s.m. A name of the bird garuṛ, q.v.
s.f. A spindle, a distaff; an iron pin upon which the cotton is first drawn out (syn. takuʼā, taklā)
subs: s.m. and tarku-pīṭhī, s.f. A ball of clay, &c., at the lower end of a spindle to arsist in giving it a rotatory motion
s.m. A small whetstone for sharpening spindles, &c.
s.m. A concave shell or saucer which serves to hold the lower end of the spindle when wheeled round.

def: s.m. The fruit of the palmyra ( = tāṛ-phal).
more: s. m. ( ताडांकुर ) The fruit of the tāṛ tree (Borassus flabelliformis).
